cannabis sativa.mechanism: THC acts primarily as a partial agonist at CB1 receptors (and CB2), while CBD has low direct CB1/CB2 efficacy and modulates endocannabinoid, serotonergic, and TRP signaling.
effects: anxiolytic or anxiogenic effects depending on dose and chemotype

banisteriopsis caapi.mechanism: Contains reversible inhibitors of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A), which reduce first-pass deamination of monoamines and orally co-administered tryptamines.

mimosa hostilis.mechanism: Contains N,N-dimethyltryptamine, a serotonergic psychedelic (primarily 5-HT2A agonist) that is orally inactive without MAO inhibition.

peganum harmala.mechanism: Beta-carbolines act as reversible MAO-A inhibitors and can potentiate monoamines and tryptamines by reducing metabolic breakdown.

lophophora williamsii.mechanism: Mescaline is a serotonergic psychedelic with principal agonist activity at 5-HT2A receptors.

erythroxylum coca.mechanism: Cocaine inhibits dopamine, norepinephrine, and serotonin transporters and blocks voltage-gated sodium channels as a local anesthetic.

coffea arabica.mechanism: Caffeine is a nonselective adenosine A1/A2A receptor antagonist that increases central catecholaminergic and glutamatergic signaling.

camellia sinensis.mechanism: Provides caffeine-mediated adenosine receptor antagonism; L-theanine co-occurrence can modulate excitatory neurotransmission and subjective stimulation profile.
contraindications: iron-deficiency risk with high intake around meals

nicotiana tabacum.mechanism: Nicotine is an agonist at neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, increasing dopamine and norepinephrine release and reinforcing use.

papaver somniferum.mechanism: Contains phenanthrene opioids that act primarily as mu-opioid receptor agonists, producing analgesia and respiratory depression in a dose-dependent manner.

valeriana officinalis.mechanism: Valerenic acid and related sesquiterpenes act as positive allosteric modulators of GABA-A receptors.

hypericum perforatum.mechanism: Hyperforin inhibits monoamine reuptake pathways and induces CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, creating substantial drug-interaction potential.
effects: antidepressant effects in mild to moderate depression

mitragyna speciosa.mechanism: Mitragynine and 7-hydroxymitragynine act at mu-opioid receptors (partial agonism with additional adrenergic/serotonergic interactions).

piper methysticum.mechanism: Kavalactones modulate GABA-A receptor function and influence voltage-gated ion channels, contributing to anxiolytic and sedative effects.

ginkgo biloba.mechanism: Terpene lactones antagonize platelet-activating factor signaling, while flavonoid fractions contribute antioxidant and microcirculatory effects.

dmt.mechanism: Agonist at 5-HT2A receptors (with activity at other serotonergic receptors); rapidly metabolized by monoamine oxidase A when taken orally without MAO inhibition.
safety: avoid with MAO inhibitors or serotonergic drugs due to interaction risk
can precipitate severe anxiety or panic
risk of psychological destabilization in predisposed individuals

thc.mechanism: Partial agonist at CB1 receptors in the CNS and CB2 receptors in peripheral/immune tissues.
safety: dose-related anxiety and paranoia can occur
impairs driving and psychomotor performance
can exacerbate psychotic symptoms in vulnerable users

cbd.mechanism: Low intrinsic efficacy at CB1/CB2; modulates endocannabinoid tone and interacts with targets including 5-HT1A and TRPV channels.
safety: can inhibit CYP enzymes and alter drug exposure
may cause somnolence or gastrointestinal upset
liver enzyme elevations reported at higher therapeutic doses

harmine.mechanism: Reversible inhibitor of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A).
safety: interaction risk with serotonergic drugs
interaction risk with sympathomimetics
may cause nausea and hypotension

harmaline.mechanism: Reversible inhibitor of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A) with CNS activity.
safety: can cause ataxia and tremor
interaction risk with serotonergic and stimulant drugs
gastrointestinal adverse effects are common

mescaline.mechanism: Primarily a 5-HT2A receptor agonist with additional activity at other serotonergic receptors.
safety: can provoke anxiety or panic
avoid in psychotic-spectrum disorders
may elevate heart rate and blood pressure

caffeine.mechanism: Nonselective adenosine A1/A2A receptor antagonist leading to increased neuronal firing and catecholamine release.
safety: can worsen insomnia and anxiety disorders
may trigger palpitations in susceptible individuals
withdrawal may cause headache and fatigue

nicotine.mechanism: Agonist at neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, increasing dopaminergic transmission in reward pathways.
safety: high dependence potential
raises heart rate and blood pressure
toxic in concentrated forms

morphine.mechanism: Full agonist at mu-opioid receptors, reducing nociceptive neurotransmission and altering pain perception.
safety: high overdose risk when combined with CNS depressants
causes tolerance and physical dependence
constipation and hypoventilation are common dose-limiting effects

codeine.mechanism: Weak mu-opioid receptor agonist; partly bioactivated to morphine via CYP2D6.
safety: respiratory depression risk in ultra-rapid CYP2D6 metabolizers
additive sedation with alcohol or benzodiazepines
dependence can develop with repeated use

mitragynine.mechanism: Partial agonist at mu-opioid receptors with additional adrenergic and serotonergic receptor interactions.
safety: dependence and withdrawal can occur
interaction risk with CNS depressants
hepatic adverse effects have been reported

7-hydroxymitragynine.mechanism: Potent partial agonist at mu-opioid receptors and major contributor to opioid-like effects of kratom preparations.
safety: respiratory depression risk increases with co-depressants
significant abuse and dependence liability
overdose risk increases in polydrug use
